$schema_note: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the W1.23 golden fixtures: it binds each synthetic fixture to the Wave 5 canary drill it drives, registers the synthetic chapter body files with their sha256 (verified against bytes by replay_golden.py), and restates the controlled vocabularies the fixtures draw from (so a drifted enum value is caught). Golden fixtures are the fixed, expected-outcome replays of the whole orchestrator ledger + assembly machine; the intake webhook battery lives separately under fixtures/webhook/ (W1.6). All data is synthetic: zero PII, zero secrets, zero client identifiers, zero Anthropic model ids, and none of the nine Downloads JSON exports in whole or in part.

fixtures.title: Full synthetic participant, S0 through approved, with one rewrite round
exercises: every stage S0..S8 and every gate
the S6 rewrite round (chapter v1 -> v2, budget 1 of 2)
a lone contributor cannot arm assembly (below min_chapters)

fixtures.title: One contact_id, two anthologies, two independent participant records
exercises: re-upsert within an anthology updates the one record (created=false)
same contact in a second anthology creates a distinct record (created=true)
independent title locks, rewrite counts, and cursors per key

fixtures.title: Exceptions-queue capture and resolve across every typed reason
exercises: capture across all five typed reasons incl. legacy_reconciliation
guard refusals: unknown reason (5), non-JSON raw (5), unknown id (3)

fixtures.title: Three synthetic chapters assembled, with the full S9 guard matrix and one exclusion
exercises: the one explicit exclusion (arms the trigger)

chapters.used_by: full-participant (S6 rewrite -> chapter v2, the frozen version)

chapter_length_note: Chapter bodies are illustrative length only. The 2,000-3,500 stripped-word CHAP-BAND floor (AF-AE-CHAP-BAND) is enforced by Skill 54's prove_aw_chapter.py against Skill 54's own golden fixtures, never against these orchestrator-ledger fixtures. Here the chapter bytes exist only so the compile-time frozen-chapter sha256 byte-identity proof (assembly-advance --verify-sha) runs against real bytes.

controlled_vocabularies_referenced.$note: Restated from anthology_state.py so a drifted enum in a fixture is caught by review. The fixtures draw ONLY from these.

exit_code_contract.2: illegal transition / guard refusal (readiness not-met, gate-bypass, second-door, unknown reason at open? no: reason=5)
exit_code_contract.5: validation, confirm-name mismatch, non-JSON raw payload, or order/sha validation

replay.isolation: each fixture replays in its own temp state dir; no base id configured -> mirror-only (SPEC 7.2), network-free
replay.canary: on the operator canary (W5.4/W5.6/W5.7) the same step sequences are exercised from BOTH doors (board card and participant token page); the intake side (T1..T9) is fixtures/webhook/ (W5.3).
